name: docxtemplater (Word/PowerPoint Template Engine)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT) core; Pro modules commercial ($500+/yr)

performance.notes: Depends on template complexity; Node.js and browser support

agent_readiness.notes: Template engine for .docx and .pptx. Designer creates Word template with {name}, {#items}{item}{/items} tags. Developer fills with data: doc.render({name: 'Alice', items: [{item: 'A'}]}). Loops, conditionals, images (pro). PizZip to load file. doc.getZip().generate({type: 'blob'}). Works in browser and Node.js. Python: docxtpl (Jinja2 for .docx). Enterprise document automation. Contrast with Carbone (PDF output).

gotchas: Pro features (images, HTML, charts) require paid modules
Complex nested loops can be tricky to template
Word template design requires Word knowledge
Template tags must be in correct XML element boundaries
best_when: Generating Word documents from templates (contracts, reports, letters)
Legal/HR document automation with designer-created templates
Enterprise document generation with existing Word templates
avoid_when: PDF output (use Gotenberg or WeasyPrint instead)
Complex layout requirements (HTML-to-PDF gives more control)
When image/chart insertion required without paying for Pro
summary: docxtemplater fills Word (.docx) and PowerPoint (.pptx) templates with data. {name}, {#items}{/items} tags in Word template. Designer creates template, developer fills with JSON. Browser and Node.js support. Loop and conditional rendering. Python equivalent: docxtpl. Pro modules add images/HTML/charts. Best for enterprise document automation using existing Word templates.
